Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the HOME-ARP bundle →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC
Read background brief →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.

0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.

Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.

2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.

Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.

Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →

Civic financial transparency · El Paso County, Colorado

Lorson Ranch Metropolitan Districts Nos. 1–7

A plain-language financial snapshot of seven cross-pledged metropolitan districts financing the Lorson Ranch community. 2026 budget year.

Total assessed value · $117.2M
2026 mill levy revenue · $8.72M
Districts · 7
Last updated · April 2026
Total residential market value
$1.62B
$1,615,898,861 — assessed at the 6.25% residential rate.
Annual mill levy collected
$8.72M
$1.46M operating · $7.25M debt service. 2026 budget.
Aggregate authorized debt
$300M
Service Plan cap. Voter authorization is $2.4B.
Combined net position (12/31/24)
−$94.2M
Aggregate audited deficit across 7 districts. MD4 alone: −$62.5M.
01

Seven districts at a glance

District No. 1 is the Service District — it operates and maintains the community. Districts 2 through 7 are Financing Districts that send tax revenue up to MD1 and pay their own bondholders.

MD No. 1

Service District

operates & maintains
Assessed value
$25,550
Total mill levy
8.750

$224
Outstanding debt
$9.84M
Status
Built out

finances · pays bonds

76.297

Outstanding debt
Series 2016
Status
Built out

76.327

Built out

2022A + 2022B

02

Mill levy by district

Most homeowners pay a combined operating + debt service mill levy. The Service Plan caps debt service at 50 mills; several financing districts exceed that under the "subject to adjustment" provision.

Operating mill levy Debt service mill levy
03

Outstanding bond debt

Six bond issues across five districts. Note the unique structure of MD1's Series 2012 bonds: 10% interest with no principal schedule until maturity in 2041. Interest accrues if pledged revenue is insufficient.

Outstanding bond debt by district
District Series Coupon Maturity Mill Levy 2026 Revenue
MD 1 Series 2012
Limited Tax GO. $9.84M outstanding (orig. $13.93M). Cash flow bond — accrued unpaid interest $3.35M and growing. 		10.00% 2041 — $0
MD 2 Series 2016
Limited Tax GO Refunding & Improvement. Pays own debt service plus $1.79M IGA cross-pledged to MD1. 		2–5% 2050 63.581 $1,470,723
MD 3 Series 2022
Limited Tax GO Refunding & Improvement. Sends $2.03M IGA cross-pledged to MD4. 		6.00% 2052 63.606 $2,373,614
MD 4 Series 2022A — Senior
Limited Tax GO Drawdown. 2026 interest expense $2.27M. 		5.50% 2057 63.238 $2,099,066
MD 4 Series 2022B — Subordinate
No dedicated mill levy. Paid from MD3 IGA + residuals. 2026: $1.16M interest + $930K principal. 		8.00% 2057 — $0
MD 6 Series 2026 Pending
Coupon and maturity TBD. Mill levy of 57.808 already certified. 2025: $50M issued + $50M developer advance repaid + $49.8M IGA to MD1 capital. 		TBD TBD 57.808 $1,311,351
04

Audited financial position

Net position from each district's 2024 audited financials. Negative figures are structurally normal for metro-district financing entities — they hold large bond liabilities while the underlying public infrastructure assets sit on the Service District (MD1) balance sheet, not theirs. The chart shows what each district owes versus what it owns, in net.

Positive net position Negative net position (liabilities exceed assets)
Audited net position by district, 2024
District Auditor Total Assets Total Liabilities Net Position
MD 1Wipfli LLP$17,465,599$14,610,453+$2,862,317
MD 2Wipfli LLP$6,290,242$16,068,430−$11,689,021
MD 3Wipfli LLP$5,387,965$26,449,758−$24,168,903
MD 4Wipfli LLP$2,830,464$62,569,213−$62,503,273
MD 5CLA (audit exempt)Total revenue $847No debtAudit exempt
MD 6Wipfli LLP$2,546,752$1,677+$1,315,435
MD 7CLA (audit exempt)Total revenue $7,690No debtAudit exempt
Combined — $34,521,022 $119,699,531 −$94,183,445
What this means: The aggregate $94.2M deficit reflects the standard accounting treatment for cross-pledged metro districts — the financing districts (MDs 2, 3, 4) hold the bond liability while the Service District (MD1) holds the depreciated capital assets the bonds funded. It does not by itself indicate distress; what matters is whether each district's pledged tax revenue stream can cover its debt service. MD1's Series 2012 bond carries the most concerning structure: $3.35M in already-accrued unpaid interest at 10%, with no scheduled principal payments until 2041 maturity. MD6 is in transition — positive at 12/31/24 with no debt, then $50M issued during 2025.
05

Where the money flows

Tax revenue moves between districts via intergovernmental agreements (IGAs). Operating revenue routes to the Service District. Debt service revenue cross-pledges between specific bond issues.

06

What does my home pay?

Calculate the Lorson Ranch district tax for a given home value. This shows district mill levy only — your full property tax bill includes county, school district, fire district, and other taxing authorities.

Your home

Edit the value to see what you'd pay each year in district mill levy.

Assessed at 6.25%: $28,125
Note: A homeowner pays only the mill levy of their specific district — not all seven combined. The figures above show what the same home would pay if it were located in each district. The full property tax bill also includes El Paso County, Widefield School District #3, Security Fire Protection District, and several smaller overlapping authorities.
07

Who sits on the boards

Each district has a five-seat board. Per the 2024 audit, board members are employees, owners, or affiliates of the developer (Eagle Development), the Landhuis Company, Tralon Homes, or holders of the districts' outstanding bonds — and acknowledge potential conflicts of interest.

Board members by district
District Director Contact On 2027 ballot

About this dashboard

Built from publicly available documents at lorsonranchmds.com: 2026 final budgets, 2024 audited financial statements, 2026 transparency notices, and the 2024 annual report. Aggregated, normalized, and presented in plain language.

All figures cross-verifiable against the source PDFs. The accompanying spreadsheet preserves the full calculation chain.

Source documents

  • 2026 final budgets — all 7 districts
  • 2024 audited financial statements
  • 2026 transparency notices
  • 2024 annual report
  • Public infrastructure debt & mill levy
  • Colorado DOLA Local Government Information System
Lorson Ranch MDs Nos. 1–7 · El Paso County, Colorado · Data as of 2026 budget cycle

Get email updates about Lorson Ranch districts

When budgets, audits, board agendas, or transparency notices are posted, we'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents. No marketing, no partners — just public-record updates.

By subscribing you agree to receive emails about Lorson Ranch metropolitan districts. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
